name: US Cash Advance
description_short: US Cash Advance at 4209 Six Forks Rd #103, Raleigh, NC 27609 in the North Hills neighborhood is Mon-Fri 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.9 stars across 16 reviews. Call +1 919-975-4163.
description_long:
US Cash Advance operates a physical location in Detroit, Michigan (20002 Schoolcraft Rd) offering quick cash loans to consumers in emergency financial situations. The company positions itself as accessible to all credit types, including those with bad credit histories, providing an alternative when traditional lending channels are unavailable. Their service model combines in-person consultations at their Detroit office with online application options, operating extended hours Monday-Friday 8am-9pm and weekends 10am-7pm.

The company offers a range of loan products including payday loans (up to $1,000), cash advances (up to $1,000), bad credit loans ($250-$50,000), same-day loans, debt consolidation, and personal loans for bad credit. All products are advertised as unsecured loans requiring no collateral, with minimal paperwork and approval for all credit types. Their stated process involves filling an online form, connection with a third-party lender, offer review, contract signing, and cash delivery.

US Cash Advance emphasizes speed and accessibility, highlighting same-day or rapid funding and a streamlined approval process. Customer reviews specifically mention processing times under 45 minutes for in-person applications and funding within hours. The company advertises transparent terms and claims no hidden fees, with staff assistance in explaining repayment obligations. Their service model appears to differentiate through in-person support and patient customer service rather than technology innovation.

The company operates within the high-cost lending space, typical of payday and cash advance businesses. While reviews are positive regarding service quality and speed, the website does not disclose APRs, fees, or other material loan terms, which is standard transparency concern for emergency lending products. The note that loans must "pass standard underwriting review" suggests credit assessment occurs despite marketing to bad credit borrowers. This is appropriate for consumers facing genuine emergencies but carries typical risks associated with high-cost short-term lending.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available with processing times reportedly under 45 minutes for in-person applications
Accepts all credit types including bad credit, with no credit check loans advertised
No collateral required for all loan products
Extended operating hours (8am-9pm weekdays, 10am-7pm weekends) including Saturday service
Staff provide patient in-person consultation and clear explanation of terms per customer reviews
Loan amounts range from $100-$50,000 depending on product, offering flexibility for different emergency levels
Online application option available in addition to in-person Detroit location
cons: Website does not disclose APRs, fees, or other material loan terms despite regulatory disclosure requirements
No information about repayment period lengths or total cost of borrowing
Customer review mentions cramped waiting area and suggests need for improved facilities
Website quality issues including repetitive content carousel and unprofessional email address (service@env-uscashadvance-compliance.kinsta.cloud)
Limited to one physical location in Detroit despite offering online services, reducing accessibility for out-of-area applicants
best_for: Consumers with bad credit or no credit history facing urgent cash needs
Individuals needing $250-$1,000 same-day or next-day funding for emergencies
People who prefer in-person consultation and support during the loan process
Borrowers unable to qualify for traditional personal loans or credit-based products
diagnosis: US Cash Advance is appropriate for consumers facing genuine financial emergencies who cannot access traditional lending and need same-day cash, particularly those with poor credit histories. The critical caveat is that the company does not transparently disclose APRs, fees, or total borrowing costs on their website, which are typically high for payday and cash advance products; prospective borrowers must contact them directly to understand the true cost before borrowing.
